method_atom: a discretized numerical simulation (using improved discretization and modern sampling algorithms on progressively larger grids and toward a limiting parameter regime) of a strongly-coupled many-component interacting field, scanning a discrete control parameter (the number of internal components) to locate the onset of a renormalization-group fixed point where the system becomes scale-invariant; the analysis detects a spontaneous symmetry-breaking transition and, critically, distinguishes an artefactual finite-temperature (thermal) transition from an intrinsic change in the system's phase structure, thereby bounding where the scale-invariant regime can begin
object_atom: quantum chromodynamics (the strong-interaction gauge theory) with eight quark flavours, its chiral-symmetry breaking in the continuum limit, and the lower edge of its conformal window
brief_reason: the transferable part is the lattice-simulation strategy of tuning a component-count control parameter to find a scale-invariant fixed point while separating thermal from intrinsic transitions; the domain-specific part is QCD, quarks, chiral symmetry and the conformal window.
